trending_topics.context: 2 posts, 17.78x baseline rate — mentioning NVIDIA, OpenAI

tweet.text:
Came across an interesting report from SVRC Research called "State of Robotics 2026", published in April. 

Which listed:

1. Figure AI 
2. Agility Robotics $CCXI
3. Apptronik 
4. $TSLA 
5. Boston Dynamics
6. Physical Intelligence 
7. 1X Technologies
8. $AMZN Robotics
9. Covariant
10. Skild AI 

As the National Champions of the United States robotics program. 

"The United States leads the world in where robotics is heading: Fundation models, OpenAI-style scaling laws applied to action, autonomous vehicles. 

While losing the race on where robotics is shipping today." 

Then it frames:

1. Rar

Fireworks is kernelmogging $NBIS and $CRWV on the exact same NVIDIA silicon. 746 tokens per second against 480 and 290.

Run the napkin math. One Fireworks GPU pushes 746 t/s while one Nebius GPU pushes 290, so a single card serves roughly 2.6x the users. To match one Fireworks rack, Nebius has to rack up around two and a half times the hardware.

Kernel optimization is the whole difference. A kernel is the low level GPU code that actually runs the model, and rewriting it lets identical silicon push far more tokens per second. Fireworks built its own through FireAttention while most neoclouds

sample_posts.text:
Comparing Fees of Crypto Cards from Etherfi, Krak, Plasma, Wirex, and KAST on €7.65 Purchase in Europe
The all-in cost after cashback:
> Etherfi cheapest at $8.48, best rate + cashback
> Kraken at $8.58, Plasma at $8.63, Wirex at $8.82
> KAST most expensive at $9.03, worst FX spread
> Same grocery run, but a 6.5% gap between best and worst, all down to FX, fees, and cashback
Source:
https://x.com/0xVishnya/status/2073734393131151637

tweet.text:
I compared 5 crypto cards on the exact same purchase in Europe. Test #4 👇

Same store, same EUR 7.65, same minute. Here's the all-in cost after cashback:

- @ether_fi : $8.48
- @Krak : $8.58
- @Plasma : $8.63
- @wirexapp : $8.82
- @KASTxyz : $9.03

Same grocery run, but a 6.5% gap between cheapest and priciest. It comes down to FX spread, fees and cashback.

Etherfi took it on the best rate plus onchain cashback. KAST charged the worst FX and a low-value fee on top.

What should I test next?

Fireworks is kernelmogging $NBIS and $CRWV on the exact same NVIDIA silicon. 746 tokens per second against 480 and 290.

Run the napkin math. One Fireworks GPU pushes 746 t/s while one Nebius GPU pushes 290, so a single card serves roughly 2.6x the users. To match one Fireworks rack, Nebius has to rack up around two and a half times the hardware.

Kernel optimization is the whole difference. A kernel is the low level GPU code that actually runs the model, and rewriting it lets identical silicon push far more tokens per second. Fireworks built its own through FireAttention while most neoclouds

the biggest risk to bitcoin is none of these things

the biggest risk is the coin being socially and memetically centered around one dude

instead of talking about monetary policy, state tyranny, scaling payments, robustness against quantum, BTCFi, privacy, block propagation or any of the other ideologically or technologically interesting things

for the past year, the entire discourse has been: "did boomer man sell or buy?"

btw, no the point is not to "not change", what you mean to say is that a monetary base must be low entropy. meaning it must be resilient against surprises (you cant build
